review_text:
I recently visited Mentabkhetna restaurant, and it was truly a great experience. From the warm and friendly greeting at the door to every dish that we ordered.
First, the atmosphere was cozy and the seating arrangements were comfortable.
We started things off with a few cold mezzas. Hummus was so creamy, baba ghanouj was flavorful, and the tabbouleh was refreshing.
For the main course, it was served hot and fresh, and the portions were generous.
Overall, I can't recommend Mentabkhetna enough. If you're looking for authentic and innovative Lebanese cuisine in a welcoming and intimate setting, this is the spot for you. I can't wait to return and try more of their delicious dishes.

address: Makaad El Mir, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: Disappointing Dining Experience at Chez Maguy
review_text:
Our visit to Chez Maguy in Batroun was a letdown from start to finish. The tabbouleh was overwhelmingly salty, the batata harra and fried fish were undercooked, and the grilled shrimp hadn’t been cleaned, leaving us to do it ourselves at the table. On top of that, the service was frustrating—an older gentleman from the staff took our salad plate before we were done and insisted it was finished, despite our objection.

We also ordered a grilled fish based on their recommendation, and while the choice itself was good, we were shocked when the bill arrived—$40 for a single piece of fish. Between the poorly prepared food, the dismissive service, and the unexpected price tag, this was not an experience I’d want to repeat or recommend.

She charged 78$ for 400gms of fish!!!! What a rip off!!! The tahini sauce was watered down and tasteless🥺 the octopus was so mushy we returned it! No desert- nothing. We will never set foot in this place again.

address: Bahsa, Batroun Lebanon
review_text:
I had one of the worst dining experiences ever at Swiss Butter in Batroun. The food was mediocre at best, but the real problem was the dishonest way they handle orders. The waitress recommended a ribeye that wasn’t even on the menu and never told us the price — which turned out to be a shocking $55 per plate. We ordered it twice, still never being informed of the cost, only to find out at the end when the bill came. This is deceptive, unethical, and unacceptable. Any restaurant with integrity tells customers the price before selling something, especially when it’s outrageously overpriced. The service was disappointing, the experience left a bad taste in every way, and I will warn others so they don’t get ripped off the way we did.

address: Batroun, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: "Exorbitant Prices and Slow Service Mar Otherwise Promising Venue"
review_text:
The venue boasts a commendable location, strategically positioned near the highway in northern Lebanon, not far from the city of Batroun. They offer an electric cart service to transport guests from the entrance to the beach, where an array of comfortable tables and chairs are available. The service, albeit courteous, is noticeably slow. The DJ requires a considerable amount of time to play invigorating music; initially, the music was exceedingly monotonous, only improving after 10:00 PM when livelier tunes finally emerged. Unfortunately, by this time, a significant portion of my friends had already left, disheartened by the earlier dull atmosphere. 

The food, while decent, is exorbitantly priced. A bottle of gin, which typically costs $25, was outrageously marked up to $128. Our modest consumption of some snacks and soft drinks, along with the gin, culminated in a staggering bill of $462. The pricing is egregiously inflated, posing a severe detriment to tourism. In numerous other countries with far superior infrastructure (such as roads, cleanliness, and service) — like Spain, France, Italy, Greece, Kazakhstan, and even Dubai — the maximum expenditure per person, including excellent food and beverages, would be approximately $40. Adding insult to injury, the valet parking demanded an additional $5.50 as a fee. In many countries, parking is complimentary when dining at a restaurant.

In Lebanon, it seems exploitation is rampant, extending beyond just the banks and political system. I intend to boycott all restaurants and pubs this summer and strongly urge others to do the same to compel these establishments to reevaluate their prices and cease their exploitative practices.

address: 13 Mina Street, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: Rude Service and Poor Quality at Oysters – Never Again
review_text:
I had a very disappointing experience at Oysters this year. We ordered grilled calamari and were served only the tentacles instead of the actual calamari, which absolutely did not match the price. When we complained, both the waiter and the manager were extremely rude, insisting “this is how we serve it.” We even showed them the restaurant’s own Instagram photos, which clearly showed a completely different dish, but the manager dismissed us by saying those pictures are just for advertising.

I visited Oysters back in 2023 and had a wonderful experience — but this time, it was the complete opposite. The fish were tasteless, the fried shrimps were served with the heads and not cleaned properly, and the calamari was a total disaster. Not only did they refuse to apologize, but the manager was unprofessional, impolite, and even threw some unnecessary words at us before walking away.

We didn’t mind paying for the dish, but we simply didn’t want it. Instead of handling the situation with professionalism, the management’s attitude made everything worse. Honestly, this visit wasn’t even worth one star.

review_text:

The staff were very nice, especially a young lady, I forgot to ask her name. They took their time to explain the dishes to us. We had Fattouch, tabbouleh, and grilled pig fish. The food was very nice, as was the lemonade.
Oysters are their specialty, so I decided to have one (my first time ever). I am sure I will try it again.

address: 13 Mina Street, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: IF IN BATROUN AND YOU SEE A RESTAURANT CALLED OYSTERS THST LOOKS SO INVITING FOR A SUNSET, DO NOT GO THERE!
review_text:
My last night in Lebanon, I go to watch the sunset and see this charming seafood place called Oysters near St Estephan Church.
I ask for oysters, the one server who spoke English said they didn’t have any, then said he would check. 10 minutes later he says yes, they have 6. I’ve eaten oysters all over the world and this place looked safe…..but it is NOT.
The oysters looked funny and had a white substance dripping off them. The server told me it was “the sauce they put on them”. Sauce? What? So I tried one with the least milky stuff on it and it tasted rancid. I spit it out into a napkin. 
I told the staff they are bad, and these were likely sitting out for hours.
They insisted I had to pay, and get this, they were 5 USDs an oyster!!! No oysters in the US are that expensive.
I was told to wait for the owner. 30 minutes later he walks up and I explained what happened. The 2 servers who served me ran off in their car. Apparently another oyster was missing. I swore I did not even eat the one and spit it out. 
He told me there was nothing he could do, he must pay for each oyster. I told him if he went to a restaurant and the food was bad, would he pay? He said “Of course”
WRONG!!!!
AVOID THIS PLACE! Maybe I was naive and stupid to try and eat oysters in Lebanon, but I have eaten many places and should’ve really been more careful, but just as a warning to others….DO NOT EAT HERE!
I usually write good reviews, but this could be one of my worst ones ever!

review_text:
This seafood restaurant stood out as a culinary revelation that defied our expectations. Despite initially being overshadowed by a couple of other higher-ranked seafood restaurants in the area, we decided to give Bistr’eau a try as our first meal, upon arriving in Batroun. Little did we know, it would turn out to be the culinary highlight of our stay, ultimately the place we selected as our last meal and the grand finale of our time in Batroun.

Situated beneath a series of picturesque succession of ornate arches along the waterfront, within one of Batroun's historical buildings at the port of the Batroûn Old Souk, this restaurant stood out as a true outlier. What made this dining experience truly exceptional was the symphony of flavors orchestrated by the fusion of cuisines, the inventive pairing of traditional and rarely-used ingredients, and the meticulous execution of each dish. Between our two visits, we embarked on a culinary journey through almost the entire menu, relishing every morsel.

The appetizers alone were a testament to the culinary prowess at play – from the refreshing Fattouch to the innovative Hummus with shrimp, the smoky allure of the aubergine with sundried tomato tarator, and fresh zaatar. The culinary exploration continued with Grilled Calamari, Sicilian octopus, shrimp fokhara with tomato sauce, and perfectly roasted Patatas Bravas. All this food only served to pave the way for the triumphant main courses—seafood risotto and the pièce de résistance, a perfectly seasoned deep-fried whole fish. The culinary symphony reached its crescendo with the Pain Perdu à la mode, offering the perfect sweet note to conclude our culinary odyssey.

review_text:
Located in Ibn Battuta mall, we visited this beautiful restaurant for late lunch. The red and white theme of the restaurant interior gives it a bright and vibrant look. Felt a very positive vibes the moment we entered the restaurant.

We started with the Functional Juices and both were very refreshing & Heavenly in taste. Start your meals with these, highly recomended. Rise and shine which is a blend of Oats, Honey, Yougurt, Berries & Bananna. Made well without excess sweet and healthy. Kale & Pineapple with is Kale, Mango, Spinach & Pineapple. This has good health benefits for those who are looking for healthy options.

Mushroom Soup was very nice consistency and very thick. We loved the taste of the spices mixed with it. The Burratta salad is no doubt another best seller. Freshly made Burratta cheese which was very soft and tasty.

Grilled shrimp skewers on a bed of Sauteed veggies is another fantastic dish. The shrimps do not smell cause the marination is perfect and we get the taste of the mild marination spices mixed with shrimps. The Turkey Club Sandwitch is another killer. Taste of the Eggs with Veggies inside is superb. Grilled salmon with Quinoa was very well done and the Salmon did not smell strong but instead we can feel the taste in our mouths. Usually a strong smell of the Salmon is a out off, so the Chef has handled this very nicely. A must have for Salmon lovers.

The Apple and Celery drink is another superb drink. All the shakes and Smoothies are made by Mary Jane a trained expert in juices.

The Strawberry Pistachio Cheesecake was the one we selected from their display of mouth watering Desserts. The tangy sauce with the sweetness underneath was a riot of taste in our taste buds. Later we had their Pistachio & Caramel Ice Creams. All these Desserts are a must have to satiate your cravings.

The restaurant supervisor Mary Jane deserve a special mention, her mannerisms and knowledge about the food were praiseworthy.

Overall we had a great experience here. Service is good. Taste, quality and presentation of the food is praiseworthy.

address: Mirdif 37th street Next to Abaya Mall, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Ryan our server did a good job with us.  The place is newish to the Mirdif area of Dubai and in the middle of the residential area.  They deliver.  The store has an American diner motif but Country Music in the background and serves European potato fries?  Not joking.

I had the Comanche Burger.  Big patty, cheddar cheese, fried onion ring, beef bacon, and their 'Black Forest' sauce that is supposed to be a mushroom and blue cheese sauce.  The burger was fresh made and served hot.  Everybody knows blue cheese makes everything 'pop'.  I couldn't taste the blue cheese at all.  This made the burger disappointing.  My partner had the Bo Burger, a standard, near homemade style America burger, and she enjoyed her fare.

All said, I might go back because everything was fresh and hot, but not likely to have the Comanche.

address: 39 26B St Mirdif, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Went for dinner yesterday after reading good reviews in zomato but it wasn’t upto mark honestly *Totally failed our expectation* - I would Highly recommend to visit KABAB KARACHI & DELHI NIHARI instead of wasting money in spice hut. Reason 👇🏻👇🏻
Ordered chicken bihari rolls, butter chicken, delhi nehari, paratha, tandoori roti & naan. 
First of all, there is no seating area as this restaurant is very small in size. One of the staff suggested us that they will serve us at car and we agreed as we were hell hungry after tiring shopping. 
1. Chicken bihari roll wasn’t spicy at all & was average in taste, nothing wow at all plus it was so small & tiny in size for 10dhs, not worth it honestly - better to go KABAB KARACHI in Al nahda as its’ spicy, tastiest, filling and worth the money (9dhs). 
2. Delhi nihari dish was so average in taste and was more of salt in it. BIG NO and better to go DELHI NIHARI as its’ delicious and more in quantity.
3. Butter chicken wasn’t bad but still not worth it as its’ less in quantity for 27dhs - better to go SINDH SHAHBAZ RESTAURANT as it is perfect in taste and quantity.
4. Now coming to paratha, tandoori roti & naan - only naan was good as paratha & tandoori roti was average in taste & size - actually all three of them are small in size for 2dhs - 2.5dhs. 
I so wanted to eat karachi rabri but after disaster experience, quit to BASKIN ROBIN. 

I wish, I would have clicked photos of their poor presentation & just three small pieces of beef in nihari but I couldn’t, was so hungry that I forgot :p  

Never visiting this restaurant for sure........

address: 38 26 B Street Mirdiff Park Center, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_title: Brunch in style right where you’re seated at!
review_text:
Journey:
We were invited to try out thier new brunch at the Mirdiff branch. Since, brunches and fridays go hand in hand in Dubai so we could not wait to experience it for ourselves.

Ambiance:
The moment you make your way towards LOL, you have a coffee & tea counter setup, where you can have a brew of your choice and right next to them are thier delicious pastries lined up. There is a casual vibe and the dim lighting to enjoy your weekend. The seating is comfy which includes couches and chairs. It is perfect either way if you are part of a large group or planning a small affair, the cafe will suit your needs.

Rating: 7.5 Golden Nuggets

Bite Board

LOL launched a new concept for the brunch where the food comes to you instead of you walking around in circles deciding what to eat. Also, it is an unlimited brunch where you can keep refilling your plates with servings of the dish you like. The brunch is priced at AED 79 and is available on Fridays and Saturdays.

Drinks: There are options for strawberry and peach lemonade, fresh orange juice and water. We tried the orange juice and strawberry lemonade. Both the drinks were refreshing.

French Toast & Pancakes: A well-cooked french toast with a dash of cinnamon and topped with fresh berries served along with maple syrup. It was our favorite dish that we could not help ourselves from a second serving, YES THAT GOOD! The pancakes were fluffy and topped with raspberry sauce but were dry texture wise.

Choice of Eggs: There are two options where you could choose between a scrambled/boiled/fried egg or a mushroom omelette. We went for a scrambled egg that was served on top of a sourdough bread.

Breakfast Rosti: A platter with chili chickpeas, bacon, cherry tomatoes, mushrooms, roasted potatoes and sausages. Our friend was a huge fan of the chickpeas dish as it was something different and had an amazing flavor to it.

Red Quinoa Salad: The combination of beetroot and quinoa was superb. Usually, we are not huge fans of quinoa but this totally changed our minds and we would not mind a repeat of it.

Moutabal: We could not taste this as there were no breads along with it. Our suggestion would be to add some arabic bread or bread-sticks in the future.

Tunisian Chicken Meatballs: A bit different from the regular meatballs which usually involves red meat. These chicken meatballs had the right amount of spices and were cooked perfectly.

Prawn Penne Arrabbiata: Since our friend was a vegetarian, we requested for the pasta without the prawns. The pasta was cooked well but we would have preferred the tomatoes to be slightly less, guess its just how the dish was and does not suit our palate.

Roast Harissa Chicken: The dish would have been another one of our favorites if only the chicken was not too dry and too hard to cut through.

Desert Platter: It included carrot cake, red velvet cake and macarons. Both the cakes were soft and melted in your mouth but the macarons were a miss for us.

Service:
This is an aspect that needs to be worked on, the staff needed to be called multiple times to hear out our request.

Cash Outflow:
The price is quite reasonable for the unlimited brunch. 

Location:
26B Street, Mirdiff Park Center, Dubai

Circling Back:
We shall make another visit to try their desserts and coffee.

address: 16, Algeria - 47th Street,, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_title: Disgusting Seating Area & Terrible Service, Rude Staff who talks about you in their native Tagalog language!!
review_text:
Not sure what I can exactly pin point here in this review because there's nothung good about Sushi Nations (Uptown Mirdif Branch) . 
Let me start with:
1) The hygiene issue: It's None Existent! Seating Area is gross, stains all over the sofas, the tables are dirty, even glasses are stained and not properly washed, probably just rinsed off before it is being used to serve another customer and that's it!!
2) The food: I can honestly say it's not good at all. It taste awful. 40% of the sushi rolls were canned tuna!!! and another 40% were crab and only 20% were veggies and salmon rolls. 
Now, the soups, they smell and taste like garbage!!
And it will take you around 1.5 hours till you get the first appetizer mini bowl. 
Then you'll have to wait 2 more hours until the sushi rolls are ready!! 
I got food poisoning when I went to their restaurant, started vomiting and had severe diarrhea right afterwards.
3) The service: When you first come in, if you're lucky, there will be 1 staff in any of the corners that will acknowledge you.
Then you pick a table, sit down and just wait and wait for a staff member to come in and take your order.
And from what it seems to me and to the group of friends I was with, that the staff don't like to work there. They are so depressed, slowly moving around and so irritated.
They talk down to you and with a loud voice. The Filipino chef there was making fun of me and my friends, IN FRONT OF ME!!! Saying demeaning things about Arabs, even though they live and work in an Arabic country, in Middle East, without even any regards or respect to me and my friends as customer and without even considering that I, maybe, speak Tagalog. 
I have actually took many courses, specifically to understand the language of many UAE residents and to call them out when they mock us, in their mother tongue, in front of us, assuming we are so oblivious and have no idea what they are talking about!!!! #SHAME

My order was:

1.       Hot and sour soup

2.       Lunch set ( Crispy chilli chicken dry, sweet and sour fish, Chicken fried rice).

3.       Lunch set (Vegetable schezwan noodles, chicken hot garlic, stir fried Chinese greens, vegetable spring rolls).

4.       Lunch set (egg schezwan rice, chicken Manchurian, stir fried Chinese greens, veg spring rolls.

Large portion size! Fried rice and noodles were generously loaded with vegetables. Mildly spiced dishes. They give some extra sauces too. Some Chinese restaurants meals contain too much salt but here food was not extra salty. Great place for desi style Chinese cuisine!

review_title: This is the first place I
review_text:
This is the first place I have seen that actually makes really good pizza and really good burgers at the same place. 

They keep the menu simple and do everything exceptional well. The pizzas are a healthy medium size with all the right ingredients and a thin floor and that flame oven taste. 

The burgers are as well on the small/medium side and really well put together. Fresh lettuce and juicy meat. What seems to be handmade sauces and well thought out combinations. 

And the fifth star is for the dynamite prawns - no idea why they are on the menu - but they are amazing. Not just some small shrimps tossed in some ready made sauce / no these are proper prawns cooked with talent and tossed in delicious sauce. 

Only for delivery and take away. Even the delivery speed was great every-time. I have and will recommend this place to everyone who loves a great pizza or a awesome burger.

address: Mirdif 35 Mall, Ground Level,, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text: Menagerie's most formidable talent lies not in the expertly(and exceptionally) prepared food, the tastefully decorated interiors or its applaudable soundtrack ( playing indie-hits like Lord Huron and Janis Ian) but rather in its ability to just 'be'. It doesn't try to propagate a faux sense of ostentatiousness or boast about its approach to healthy living. In this exclusive little mall called Mirdif 35, Menagerie is a place untouched by the too-easily damaged veneer of golden consumerism that plagues 99% of tinsel Dubai. As a regular customer, and a fan of everything from their raw doughnuts(vegan) to almond-milk lattes with agave and the refreshing acai bowl. Menagerie 35 exists to better your day, or at least that has been my personal experience. It is also worth mentioning that it is one of the only cafes where one can read a good book, unbothered.

address: Al Mizhar 43c St, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Hatam __ A lovely Iranian Restaurant in Dubai! They serve complimentary bread and green salad platter that consist of rocket leaves,mint leaves,walnuts, olives,feta cheese, and pickled veggies upon arrival.

I used cobone vouchers for my meal. This cobone deal is still on. You can save upto 50% on the bill by getting the vouchers. The menu card prices available on Zomato website for this restaurant need some updating as these are different from the prices mentioned on the Menu card at the restaurant.

I had the following dishes:

Lentil soup:  It was non spicy and tasted good.

Mixed salad: Though they serve basic salad platter complimentary but this 4 in 1 platter was really good. It had hummus, mutable, tabbouleh and seasonal salad in it.

Mixed Hatam salad: consisted of  dolma, humus topped with grilled meat, feta cheese and green mix salad. Loved it!

Arayes: Arayes is a middle eastern grilled pita bread stuffed with lamb mince, onions, tomatoes and spices. It was very delicious! But they forgot to serve fries with it as it was shown in the menu card’s Arayes picture.

Kabab Koobideh:  Special Iranian kababs served with rice were very juicy and tasty. They make the best koobideh kababs!!

Tikka and kabab:  Marinated lamb pieces served with rice were nicely grilled.

Falooda:  It was very chilled and refreshing!

Overall it was a nice experience. Seating facility is good. Walls are adorned with beautiful paintings. They also have family cabins for privacy. Our server was kind and friendly. He was happy to help us when we asked him for the extra plates and cutleries. I would love to come back here again.

#littlepakistanikitchen #instafoodie #dubaifoodies #arabiancenter

address: Rue Martyr, Bcharre Lebanon
review_text: Very nice lunch at this restaurant, the food was tasty and the service extremely friendly and professional. Très adapté aux familles également

address: Rue Martyr, Bcharre Lebanon
review_text: Never fall into hesitation in passing by for the warmest greeting coming from the staff, any choice of platers is amazing, well garnished and highlighted with yummy taste that leaves your stomach full.  will see you soon guys

address: Rue Martyr, Bcharre Lebanon

address: Arz Street Inside Samar Resort, Bcharre 961 Lebanon
review_text: The food was nice and the place is really calm with a good atmosphere. Just my food took ages to come to my place, and I realized they forgot my order. We were alone in the restaurant.

address: Arz Street Inside Samar Resort, Bcharre 961 Lebanon
review_title: Great food for a break from Lebanese food
review_text: Kids wanted something other than Arabic food. This food was great and the owner was super kind and fluent in English. Highly recommend!!

address: Arz Street Inside Samar Resort, Bcharre 961 Lebanon
review_text: Simplicity at its best! Friendly service, great quality of food, delicious taste, wide variety. Marwan's hospitality will make you feel right at home. A must try :)

address: Arz Street Inside Samar Resort, Bcharre 961 Lebanon
review_text: I had the fish burger. It tasted good, but after a couple of hours I turned very sick with food poisoning and have been sick for 2 days now...

address: Arz Street Inside Samar Resort, Bcharre 961 Lebanon
review_text: More or less as a coinicident we stepped by but the stop made our day. The coffee was excellent - you can tell that the owner spent years in Italy... Unfortunately, we had no time to eat (even though it looked tempting) but that was due to the outstanding hospitality of the owner: As he heard that we wouldnt have enough time to see the famous cedars, he spontanously offered us a ride, so that we made it in time for the last bus to Beirut. Overall: The Masa is definitely worth visiting, you can be sure to support fantastic people and a superb family-run business!

address: Maroun Chalfeh St, Bcharre Lebanon
review_text: Feel right at home for a truly heartwarming Lebanese experience. The food is out of this world, and make sure you have a shisha. The hosts are so accommodating and kind. Food is Jim-made. Feel welcome and so at home at this place.. very true to the name which translates to my house is your house.

address: Maroun Chalfeh St, Bcharre Lebanon
review_title: Amazing Food and Great Family Restaurant
review_text: Eating at L Bayt Baytak was one of the best meals I had in Lebanon while I was there. The food was really delicious and stuck to both the traditional styles of Lebanese cuisine and current styles of cooking.The atmosphere of the restaurant is really homely. L Bayt Baytak offers an outdoor dining setting, with plastic screening to stop annoying insects getting in. The scenery around the sitting area is really beautiful, with gorgeous wild daisies growing nearby. The service is great, and the staff were really nice.The facilities are good, with a male and female toilet. I would definitely recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a good feed, especially families.

review_text: Delicious food great service good atmosphere.... i really really love it i recommend it  to every one to visit that place 👌

address: Kadisha Valley Rahme Building, Bcharre Lebanon
Cedars - Main Road, Bcharre 1377 Lebanon
Ariz Main Road Facing Cedars Forest, Al Arz 0096 Lebanon
review_text:
Behara has hospitality experience in Switzerland and Dubai that I know of. He took over the extended family restaurant that has been around for 82 years a year ago when he bought out his uncles. He's converted it into a Lebanese/International locale with abundant charm! 

His mother makes the kebnehnayyeh fresh each morning and his dedication and passion are clearly evident. Everything we ate was fresh and served just right. If you're nice he may even bless you with a drop of his homemade vodka with cedar sap. Retsina like but so much better 🤩

Don't miss stopping here for any meal including breakfast and don't miss a walk through the park across the street to see the incredible cedar trees. It's worth the drive without a doubt. Bravo!!🙌

address: Ariz Main Road Facing Cedars Forest, Al Arz 0096 Lebanon
review_text: The menu is good compared to the current situation in Lebanon. The quality is acceptable and the view is superb.

address: Ariz Main Road Facing Cedars Forest, Al Arz 0096 Lebanon
review_text:
One of the best places to eat at the cedars forest, been eating here for many years and always return and leave with a smile on my face. Will definitely recommend it to my friends. 

Not to forget the best cedar honey in the area. I shall always return.

address: Ariz Main Road Facing Cedars Forest, Al Arz 0096 Lebanon
review_text: the food and service was excellent in addition of a really friendly staff and a great view. I recommended for family and couples.

address: Ariz Main Road Facing Cedars Forest, Al Arz 0096 Lebanon
review_title: Very good experience. Highly recommended. Enchanting place and delicious food.
review_text: Authentic Lebanese food. Offering a wonderful variety of the traditional mountain dishes including the famous lebanese mezzeh and freshly hot man2oucheh… overlooking the millennium cedar forest. Highly recommended

address: Al-Dawalib Main Street, Bcharre 3934 Lebanon
review_title: Excellent! The food, the view, the staff very friendly
review_text:
The food is top 
The staff very friendly
The view is beautiful 
They recommended us many things that were delicious specially the meat

address: Al-Dawalib Main Street, Bcharre 3934 Lebanon
review_text: Amazing view great food the restaurant is overlooking kadisha valley, with a stunning view the food is freshly homemade, very kind owners. A must try .

address: Al-Dawalib Main Street, Bcharre 3934 Lebanon
review_title: Authentic Homecooked Lebanese Food in a Beautiful Setting
review_text:
This restaurant is a must visit when coming to Bcharre. The service, food, setting and views from their lovely veranda were all absolutely tip top. 
The owners could not be more welcoming and accomodating. I cant remember the last time I recieved such attentive and personal service in a restaurant. The moutabal and Harra potatoes were my favourite although all the food was delicious. Lebanese cuisine at its finest. We are looking forward to visiting River Rock again on our next visit to Bcharre.

address: Al-Dawalib Main Street, Bcharre 3934 Lebanon
review_title: A one of a kind restaurant
review_text:
What an awesome restaurant located on top of wadi qadisha (vally of qadisha). The food was very delicious and it comes with a variety of side dishes and authentic Lebanese appetizers. You will experience a very welcoming atmosphere with Lebanese music played in the background while you explore different dishes and appetizers. Having your lunch here is a must if you find yourself wandering nearby. Don’t forget to taste every single dish cuz the more you try Lebanese food, the more you’ll fall in love with this fascinating country.

Un repas exceptionnel vous attend dans ce restaurant. Il donne sur un lieu sacré qui est considéré l’un de premier lieux de pilgrimage au liban. Vous allez recevoir de l’énergie positive dès que vous arrivez ici. N’oubliez pas d’essayer chac plat et chaque apéro pour que vous puissiez avoir une expérience totale de la cuisine libanaise

address: Arez/Cedars Highway, Bcharre Lebanon
review_text: The view is breathtaking! The food is one of the best I have had the last 10 days in Lebanon. The service is excellent. And of course it’s a short drive to Cedars of Lebanon. A perfect destination. Highly recommend it as part of your visit to our beautiful country 🇱🇧♥️

address: Arez/Cedars Highway, Bcharre Lebanon
review_title: The best way to finish the day
review_text: excellent view, servcie and food with reasonable prices. Right below the Cedars forest the restaurant overlooks Bshari twon and the mountains. it is also surounded by fruit trees, we enjoyed the cherries

address: Arez/Cedars Highway, Bcharre Lebanon
review_text: Amazing view! Five stars food, surrounded with fruit trees mainly cherries, friendly staff, fresh juices, loved it

address: Arez/Cedars Highway, Bcharre Lebanon
review_text: Located between the Cedars of God and Bcharre, this small outdoor restaurant sits on top of the Qadisha Valley and boasts one of the most expansive views of the area. Due to its outdoor location, Cedars Heaven is only open during the summer.

address: Arez/Cedars Highway, Bcharre Lebanon
review_text: We had lunch at Cedars Heaven.  It's a no frills place - nice view of the valley but the restaurant had plastic chairs and tables with torn plastic tablecloths.  Food was average, nothing special.  Drinks were served in plastic cups. The road to get there was unpaved and it was a bit of a bumpy ride.  There is a small (structure) of a church just beside the restaurant that you can walk up to.

address: Nabeh Mar Sarkis Road, Bcharre Lebanon
review_text:
Popped in to London for a shopping trip and decided we should eat something before shopping. So chosen this place because I came here a couple of years ago. Polite welcoming staff members and the food was pretty good as well.
Unfortunately we had to use their toilets and this really let them down on cleanness. 
I fully understand that religion may have something to do with the toilet being that messy but surely something can be done by keeping on top of the mess.

address: Nabeh Mar Sarkis Road, Bcharre Lebanon
review_text: Been there last week, good swift service regardless of the current economic crisis, staff were helpful and allowed us to change for a photo-shoot. impressive decoration yet lacks the final touch, the owner is very friendly, the place is well heated compared to the freezing temperature outside. The location is way more than perfect.

address: Nabeh Mar Sarkis Road, Bcharre Lebanon
review_title: Great Restaurant in a Picturesqu Village
review_text:
This is a nice restaurant in a remote, picturesque small village. It is close to big cedar and the cedar forest which gives the village its name.
The interior is quite big and big tables are laid out.
The food is typically Lebanese and is substantial. They also serve alcohol.
Service was very good and quick but we had phoned ahead.
A very good typically Lebanese restaurant.

review_text: We went there for lunch with our kids. One of their staff decided to mock our kid and curse him out of sudden . Shockingly the management rather than trying to solve the issue they considered what happened as a normal act.

address: Old Souk, Pepe Abed Street Facing historical Castle main gate, Byblos Lebanon

address: Old Souk, Pepe Abed Street Facing historical Castle main gate, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Lunch at Locanda a la Granda in Byblos
review_text: Serving fusion-style cusine and specializing in redesigning famous traditional Lebanese and international dishes, Locanda a la Granda occupies a prime spot in Byblos old souk. Enjoy the view from the terrace while you enjoy the delicious food and restaurant's special l Locanda Beer.

address: Old Souk, Pepe Abed Street Facing historical Castle main gate, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
So, my family and friends are visiting lebanon l, not for the the first time.  We have been to many places and restaurants,  and everyone was great and nice to us.  

After touring around the area,  we decided to go to this place to have a drink,  eat some appetizers and sweet dishes. When we entered the place,  they didn't say any welcome greetings. We wanted to place an orders,  but after knowing that we don't want to eat main dishes,  he told us that he is "not interested in only serving us if we don't order main dishes". Then he told us to "leave and go somewhere not here" in front of everyone.  Everyone was looking, and it was super embarrassing. 

I will never bring anyone to this place ever. The way we were treated and possibly racially profiled wasn't OK. I will bring my family and friends who are coming to lebanon to better places. 

You can never treat anyone like that ever!!

address: Old Souk, Pepe Abed Street Facing historical Castle main gate, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: Terrible waiter  and customer service  food is good but overall bad experience ruined the evening  we will never recommend it

address: Jbeil, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
It is a lovely cafe, perfect for gatherings with your friends. 

Comfy sofas, a cup of coffee and you feel liken you are in New York sitting in  central perk, waiting for Chandler or Joey from Friends to open the door. 

Also a lovely place for read, write or study.

address: Jbeil, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: Visited the place a week ago. It was quite nice, with indoor and outdoor seating which is great. Smoking is allowed outside. The coffee is good and the ambience is very chilled. Loved the music a lot great oldies era. You can take a stroll in Jbeil after your cup of coffee. Recommended 👌

address: Rue Pepe Abed, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: I got a very bad experience!! Im a tourist from kuwait i went there to have a dinner with the family the girl who is working their was very ruude with us when we enter the restaurant without any reason!! and we decide to left the place without ordering anything .

address: Rue Pepe Abed, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Feniquia is exceptional.
Amazing service with incredibly warm and professional staff, extraordinary food full of flavor and passion, and a beautiful atmosphere that makes the whole experience unforgettable. Truly one of the best restaurants — highly recommended.

address: Rue Pepe Abed, Byblos Lebanon

address: Rue Pepe Abed, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Horrible service. Food came wrong or very late.
review_text: Very poor experience. One third of our orders came out wrong and had to be returned to be redone. It took 45 minutes to make baked potatoes. We waited for an hour after most of us were done eating for a shawarma sandwich order for my teenage son. Eventually, after many “3 more minutes madame”, I sent the boys with my husband to have a tawook sandwich at a restaurant next door. All this, with the restaurant mostly empty..Horrible service. Horrible experience.

address: Rue Pepe Abed, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: Nicely presented plates either hunging from the ceiling or served on charcoal, big variety to chose, freshly cooked and a nice surprise at the end: on the house after 8 flavoured ice cream. Shots of arak served for all also complimentary

address: Edde Yard Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:

address: Edde Yard Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: eCafe, in the center of Byblos, has Mediterranean atmosphere and delicious international menu.
review_text: ECafe has a friendly and helpful chef and staff who provide  delicious meals with Middle Eastern flair. I spent a delightful Thanksgiving dinner in the private dining room.

address: Edde Yard Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Beautiful setting with European/North American food
review_text: We only had drinks here after a stroll through the Souks so I can’t speak to the food - but the setting is truly lovely and refreshingly quiet after the bustling shops and boiling hot castle. Lovely for a pit-stop.

address: Edde Yard Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Not what you expect but in a nice way
review_text:
Byblos is full of Lebanese cuisine and this is completely different. The speciality is really BBQ steaks and grills of all sorts along with potatoes and salads. In a sense more North American or European style.

Excellent quality steaks, cooked on a wood fired BBQ to perfection along with roasted potatoes, salads and a delicious mushroom sauce to go with the steak.

Also a very good selection of Lebanese wines. The Reds go well with the steaks.

Deserts were good as well, especially the fresh waffles, chocolate ice cream and strawberries.

address: Edde Yard Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: The place is located in the heart of the Souk. The overall experience is great. The food was so delicious, the staff is friendly and helpful. They offered us desserts because they forgot to bring our salad. That was a nice gesture!

address: Le Phoenix De Byblos Public Beach, Byblos 000000 Lebanon
review_text: Great seaside location. The best music vibe, service, yummy drinks and munchies. Reasonably priced. Unforgettable sunset views. Just delightful.

address: Rue 30 old town byblos Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
The place is nice having a nice music and great DJ.

address: Rue 30 old town byblos Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: My favorite destination when in lebanon! Their bartender is one of the best ive ever met! Must try!!

address: Rue 30 old town byblos Old Souk, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: We we're walking around and decided to stop for a drink. We were immediately welcomed by bartender and the owner of the place. They told us a bit about the area and served the drinks. They didn't have exactly what I asked however they came up with alternative which was highly appreciated! Unfortunately we didn't visit the place in the evening but I'm sure they have fantastic ambience during the night hours by having DJ playing. Great location, heartfelt experience that worth to be tried!!! We will definitely come back 😊

address: Main Old Souk road Old Souk, Byblos 7111 Lebanon
review_text: Very friendly atmosphere, quality spirits and cocktails, cold beer, on ressonable price . Paul is there to welcome you.

address: Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
The food is excellent, the pulled half chicken tasted great but it was over burned, also the shush Tawook, same thing,
We ordered some appetizers,
We got everything as it should, but the grilled cheese rolls, came too late, after we nearly finished our main course.

At the end they brought us some nice ice cream as a complimentary dessert,
Overall experience was good.
To be repeated often

address: Mestita Highway, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
I loved the idea of having three restaurants in one place, you can order whatever you want from three different menus. 
We ordered a pizza, a shawarma and a burger, all of them were so delicious!
I also loved the ambiance and the decoration.
I recommend everyone to visit this place!

review_text: Beautiful restaurant very good food , friendly staff we spent nice evening there with family. I advise this restaurant , nice garden .

address: Main Street, Bikfaya Lebanon
review_text:
Went with friends. To the spot they've taken In LOLA in al NAAS for the summer months. 

Nice garden seating with Pizza Oven in corner. 

Ordered 

Margherita Pizza. Made fresh in front of us and tasted great. 

Truffle Gnocchi. Very good and perfectly cooked. 

Edamame. Biggest portion I have ever seen served. Lol

Bruschetta which was great and fresh. 

Burrata which was cold and fresh and great. 

I wanted the Salmon Tartare but unfortunately it was not available that night. 

And Pain Per Du for dessert. One of the best I have had. 

Will return. 
Will recommend. 

Thx Guys.

address: Main Street, Bikfaya Lebanon
review_text: Its the best italian restaurant in lebanon,the risoto au pesto is the best risoto i've ever tasted in my life

review_text: Baylassan is a great restaurant and place that you don’t want to miss especially on Thursday night since they have a fayrouzyiet and old Lebanese songs live show. The restaurant is so beautiful and big. They have indoor and outdoor seating. I am not a hooka’s fan, but actually I had one at this place, and I loved it. Service was slow, but consider how crowded it was on that night, it still manageable. The singer and the band are excellent. Food is very delicious. Environment is so friendly and welcoming. Highly recommended.

review_text: The best beachfront restaurant in lebanon with amazing view and great food.we went there for lunch and the service was outstanding. I recommend everyone to try it

address: Byblos Lebanon, Byblos Lebanon

review_text: One can enjoy a vast array of Lebanese food items and fresh sea food specialties with the most beautiful view and ambience. Prices, service , staff and hygiene are also highly amazing .

review_title: Food and service need to improve
review_text:
The food is unfortunately not very satisfying. The meat is chewy and overcooked for lamb, some of the mezze (octopus with provincial sauce) is just over spiced and has extra lemon in it, you loose the initial taste of it, the fwerigh with tomato sauce were tasteless. Hummos and salad were average. Service is slow although we were the only customers at that time. And of course you ask for something and get something else kind of mistakes. 
This venue has a lot of improvements to make

review_text: Great food and good service. It is a newly opened place so the servers weren’t that great with knowing what was on the menu but they did a good job even though there was a lot of people and had a lot to do.

review_text:

address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Tasty food just outside of the souk
review_text:
The restaurant is just outside the souk and very popular with local families..the decor is not great and most waiters only speak Arabic. However the food is very fresh and tasty. Chicken skewer was perfectly cooked, juicy and not dry.. price is fair.
Overall it’s a great alternative to all the overpriced tourist restaurants serving bland food in the souk

address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: This place is for you to grab your meal quickly. It is always crowded because it has really good food. But the dessert is much better than normal food!

address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon

review_text: Very good food, good service, kind staff, not expensive. Only good things to say.  They have inside A/C seating and outside seating. Their ice cream is amazing. Their fruit cocktails are VERY good. I love the pizza, the salads, the chawarma...

address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon

review_text: I used to eat Chicken shawarma almost every week from this restaurant. I ordered last week a delivery for me and my friends ( I advised them to order from there) and it was really terrible, they couldn’t eat their sandwiches. Really disappointed

address: Mastita - Drive Thru, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Nour and Ahmad dawalibi, best Baristas ever, so friendly always smiling...

address: Voie 13, Centre Laura GF, near Byblos bank Facing Salameh, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: One of the best organic shops and cafés in Lebanon
review_text: I’m always looking for nice places to work wherever I go, comfortable to sit and hang-out, not too noisy, naturally well-lit, with delicious coffee and food if possible... I’m glad I’ve found the perfect place when I’m in Byblos, Lebanon: OSOM Organic Café and Shop. It’s so peaceful, with zen music and lemongrass incense smell, comfortable sitting area, delicious carrot cake and brownies, lots of vegan options, fresh lemonade and carrot juice, all organic and at an affordable price... The perfect place for freelancers to work, eat, and even shop.

address: Voie 13, Centre Laura GF, near Byblos bank Facing Salameh, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: An outdoor pet friendly coffee, organic place, in jbeil...
review_text: In the heart of a city, you ll find a little peaceful garden, with everything healthy, that u imagine.. Beautiful music and the great smile of the owner... A place to visit, and enjoy..

address: Voie 13, Centre Laura GF, near Byblos bank Facing Salameh, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Amazingly decorated shop, with various organic products. 
My favourite spots are the seating area outside and the couch for reading or working upstairs.
Friendly and helpful staff.

address: Byblos, Byblos 961 Lebanon
review_text: A decent place for low cost food with great taste. The menu is limited to fast made food, however tasteful and you are not hungry when you leave.

address: Byblos, Byblos 961 Lebanon
review_text:
We checked out this place following a recommendation by locals. It’s very close to the main touristy area of Byblos(Jbeil), but remains off the beaten path. If not walking there from the main souk and sites, the Uber ride is an affordable 4$! It’s along the main road to the Saint Charbel Monastery.

This is where the locals go for fresh, clean, affordable and tasty Lebanese food. We were two and split the fattouch (delicious), hummus, and the mixed grill. Everything is of top quality and the soujouk kebabs were particularly excellent. 

We would definitely come back and recommend Kabablaki as a place to check out and eat where locals eat.

We ate at the restaurant and it seemed to attract a lot of delivery orders (takeaway).  I  believe they have convenient hours since it stays open late.

address: Byblos, Byblos 961 Lebanon
review_text:
Ordered a delivery of some grilled chicken and a tabboule: the delivery arrived in 45 minutes, the food was in adequate temperature, the portions are generous and the prices are good. Overall recommended.
PS: Tastiest garlic paste I've had in a while

address: Byblos, Byblos 961 Lebanon
review_text: If you’re looking for a place to try out Lebanese food/grill then Kabablaki is the best place in Jbeil!! Very friendly service, yummy food and fair prices

address: Byblos, Byblos 961 Lebanon
review_title: Great lebanese barbecue with good prices
review_text: The restaurant is such a nice one to visit in Jbeil area in order to have a delicious Lebanese meal with very fair prices!

address: Crossroad 13th Avenue, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:

address: Jbeil - Aannaya Road, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Ksaar (for me and my family) is an annual must have dining every year we vacation in Lebanon.

The cuisine is plentiful with a wide variety of choices from Lebanese / Italian & generally Mediterranean options including grilled and seafood platters.

Kids menus are also available with a bonus playground onsite and monitored by restaurant staff, however, parental or nanny supervision is highly recommended for 
young children till the age of 7 in my opinion.

The local delicacies such as sujok and mhamraa, and kubbeh are fantastic with a glass of beer ;) Pizza's, fries and burgers are also available along with pasta  

Ksaar also has outdoor seating for those with a need of a hooka bubbly.

I recommend Ksaar and rate it highly.

address: Old Port Houssami bldg, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
Absolutely disappointing experience at El Molino.
Nothing tasted fresh — from the pork to the chicken, everything felt off. The pork lacked that juicy, flavorful taste you’d expect, and the chicken was just as underwhelming.

The tortilla bread had a moldy smell, which was honestly unacceptable. When we pointed it out, the staff took their time to respond and didn’t even bother to ask if the replacement was okay.

The service was careless and indifferent. No follow-up, no apology — it’s like they just didn’t care.
We left feeling disappointed and honestly a bit grossed out. Definitely not coming back.

address: Old Port Houssami bldg, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
El Molino – A Slice of Mexico in the Heart of Byblos
Tucked in the charming old town of Byblos, El Molino is a refreshing and vibrant escape that brings the bold and colorful flavors of Mexico to the Lebanese coast. From the moment you step inside, you’re greeted by warm hospitality and a festive atmosphere that blends Mexican décor with a Mediterranean twist.
The menu is thoughtfully curated, offering a range of traditional Mexican dishes—from sizzling fajitas and loaded nachos, to authentic tacos al pastor, burritos, and enchiladas. Every dish bursts with flavor and is clearly crafted with fresh, quality ingredients. One standout is the guacamole, freshly made tableside and served with crispy homemade tortilla chips. Their margaritas—especially the spicy version—are not to be missed, offering the perfect balance of heat and citrus.

Service is friendly and attentive, with staff happy to explain dishes or offer recommendations. The ambiance is lively yet cozy, ideal for both casual outings and special celebrations. Whether you’re seated indoors or enjoying the outdoor terrace, the vibe remains inviting and relaxed.

We were walking around Jbeil at around 12 noon. Too early for lunch but wanted just to sit for coffee. Some guy there invited us to sit at sultan al mina. From the minute we stepped in we knew we made the wrong choice but still thought for a coffee with a sea view cant really be too bad. But it was.  The place was almost empty. At the entrance was a couple of cats eating into a plastic bag. Turned out it was the fish just delivered which will be later served to clients.  We sat down and the floor under the table was dirty. address: Port Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: undoubtedly the best fatoush of my life so far
review_text: We stumbled on this place due to their signs by the roadside. What luck and what a revelation. We had a set meal: the hummus was perfect, the babaganoush was to die for. Because we were wondering about it, we were shown how they smoked the aubergine. The fatoush was just incredible and contained such a variety of herbs leaves, some of which we didn't recognise. A perfect balance. Then long double cooked chips, calamari which was cleanly crisp outside and succulent tasty with no rubberiness. It was served with a variant of tartare sauce, pleasantly acidic. Then we had a variety of crisply fried smallish fish. Utterly delicious, too much for me. When we thought this was over, they brought us a delicious, crisp fried sweet pie, like a rose water flavoured Greek galactoburico. We were in heaven. it is all fresh, much of it home produce, the owners are fishermen. It cost less than half the price of restaurants on the harbour, yet this is just yards from the sea and so friendly.  One of those finds I would not tell you about if I lived more locally!

address: Port Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: Our driver bought us here for lunch as he knew the owners. The restaurant was empty but clean and well maintained. They put on a fabulous spread of mezze and fresh seafood. (They didn’t give us a menu the driver asked them to bring us a range) Everything was fresh, well seasoned and delicious. The bill was $90 total for three of us with soft drinks but we had so much food we could justify the price. Thank you Amal for cooking such a lovely home cooked meal.

address: Port Street, Byblos Lebanon

address: 37 Rue Vieux Port, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: A charming dessert spot in Byblos
review_text: Un Petit Gato is a charming dessert spot tucked away in the heart of Byblos. With its elegant lavender facade and cozy interior, it offers a welcoming atmosphere perfect for a sweet break. The selection of pastries is both refined and delicious, blending French-inspired treats with a local touch. A must-visit for dessert lovers seeking quality, aesthetics, and a peaceful vibe, and the owners are very friendly ❤️, love the place, a second visit is a must

review_text:
Just to tell you that before working in taxi business I was a restaurant director for 12 years).

I tried before this restaurant and it was one of my preference. 3 weeks ago I decided to invite someone for a small lunch ... because we have a business meeting after one hour .
My friend asked for a pizza and one beverage and I asked for shish taouk on tabliyeh ... 20mn later my friend got his pizza ... and me ??? nothing.
Before finishing his pizza , my friend proposed me to eat the last slide of the pizza ... I did because I was hungry... What about my order : Nothing..
Guys... Normally when  we  2 persons enter a restaurant to eat, they shall eat together ... right?
As I learned during my career that the Client is a KING.. In this case the KING became ... ?
.... I told them that I don't need anymore my order.. because the business meeting is in 10 mn .. and I asked for the bill ...
The director or maybe the owner* bring the bill without any apologies.... This is the KING that I am
... and this is the professionalism that he is ..
I paid with pleasure... but the note if I can put is  - 10  .. and NEVER AGAIN...

We were walking from the Old Souk and looking for a place to eat that didn’t overlook the site of Ancient Byblos. Strolled by here and decided to drop in because it didn’t have an English name. It turned out to be a good choice.
  A very nice waiter came quickly over to the table with menus. There is quite a lot to choose from, but we decided on a salad for my wife and a mixed meat platter for us to share. Our food arrived quickly and we were pleased. It turned out to be a great stop. 
  We know food is a difficult subject to review. A restaurant is only as good as the last meal served, but this one pleased us. Nice staff, the food came quickly, and it tasted great.

address: Seaside Road Jbeil District, Amchit Lebanon
review_title: Rude owner, careless, insulting and a bully
review_text:
My partner and I like to sit next to each other so we talk quietly and share a nice experience. The owner approached us towards the end of our lunch and told us change our seating habit, this is after we finished eating and are just chilling enjoying our time. We were taken by that comment and chose to leave a few moments later. There were 4 men behind us downing scotch, talking loud on their phones while eating, nothing wa said about them. We paid and left but in retrospect lots of actions could have taken. Instead of making a scene, we called the owner on the way asking for reasons for his tone, he said "it's my restaurant and I can do as I chose, and for us not to come back again, hangs up the phone" 

Could be an identity crisis issue he is facing  but if u ask me and you are a respected client, specially a woman, the owner may have some issues...

A time like this when we support our local businesses, only to be insulted, disrespected, and humiliated in front of others with no real reasons given, I can only phathom why some men behave this way towards women in this day and age.

address: Seaside Road Jbeil District, Amchit Lebanon
review_title: A gem seafood restaurant in Amchit
review_text: This is a very small restaurant that is part of a fishery on the coast of Amchit north of Byblos. The fishery is well known under the name of its owner Pierre Abboud but few people know that it is also a restaurant. The place is very small (8 tables only) and there is no decoration. Actually you can’t even see it from the road because it is hidden by a tree. But the food is so good. Of course, since Pierre is a fisherman you get the catch of the day. All fresh and perfectly cooked  and seasoned. We had a selection of calamari with garlic and parsley, fried fish and broiled sole. Accompanied by hummus and fattoush. It was all delicious. Service is fast. And the surprise: prices are way cheaper than the trove of fancy seafood restaurants dotting the Byblos-Amchit coast. Definitely a hidden gem.

review_text: I had a terrible experience at this sushi restaurant. I found a hair in my sushi, which is completely unacceptable. address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
As a Byblos resident living 5 min away from Mon Maki, I would caution against visiting this restaurant due to the alarming inconsistency in their food quality. The last time I ordered for pick-up, the sushi was horrible, and I'm not exaggerating when I say "horrible". Specifically, I ordered sushi rolls that included salmon, mango, and avocado. The mangos and avocados were not ripe at all. I'm talking apple textured mangos and rubber textured avocados. It's unbelievable that they could even have the audacity to serve them like that. Almost as if it was done on purpose. And to top it off, the rice was also undercooked, resulting in a horrible overall experience. We were 3 people and we couldn't eat more than two pieces.
I don't usually write reviews for restaurants, but with their ridiculous prices, I felt the responsibility to share my honest experience and warn other people. Especially after reaching out to the restaurant to express my concerns, and all they did was reply to me with "we'll call you back in a bit" (they never did btw).

review_text: We had a dinner for two me and my wife, the service was excellent, we were welcomed first by a guy name Mazen and he was extraordinary & generous when he offered complimentary   fresh lemonade , later we served the food by a very warm waitress names Clarita who was helpful and attentive in every detail, the lasagna was out of this world (oven baked) , the margarita pizza was yummy as well , but the icing on the cake was the tiramisu 🤤 a must try hidden gem when you visit the area

address: Mraba'a Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Hats off, from a usually fussy client!
review_text: The overall best place in Byblos if you like authentic, fresh and delicious Italian cuisine. This is the WOW place, I would say. Aesthetically a joy for the senses. The beautiful decor, the comfortable chairs, a cozy lighting and a busy open kitchen! You would commit a sin if during your stay in Byblos you don't have at least one meal here. I had a delicious freshly made Tiramisu here with an ice Americano. The Tiramisu is a one way ticket to heaven! Then I spotted a waiter grinding fresh pesto to go with freshly made bread, another work of magic. Highly attentive, courteous yet professional servers. It was delightful to see a number of tables celebrating reunions inside and on the outside patio of the restaurant. A kids' cubby house with a slide would keep parents happy and kids safe while healthy and fresh food is served. I wish I was staying longer in Byblos to sample their entire menu. Hats off, from a usually fussy client!

address: Mraba'a Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: A dreamlike restaurant straight out of a fairy tale! The service is wonderful, attentive, and fast – you instantly feel like you’re in Italy. The food, the atmosphere, and the staff deserve more than 10 out of 10. Our waiter Serg was incredibly kind and made us feel so warmly welcomed. We’ll definitely be coming back!
